summary: The reference context identifies all relevant code areas and the code-level observations are well-researched. However, four critical problems block approval: (1) the key requirements for runtime/exception.md are entirely wrong — the actual spec's requirements R1-R7 cover class hierarchy, instantiation, matching, thread-local state, ExceptionGroup, except*, and symbol registration; none cover exception chaining; the reference context describes source code implementation state, not spec content; (2) the key requirements for hir-to-mir.md are wrong — the actual spec's requirements R10/R11/R12 cover __name__ initialization, not Raise lowering dispatch; (3) the spec plan is severely incomplete — only one entry for a feature that requires changes in at least three areas (runtime, testing, and traceback display); (4) the spec plan sections 'overview, changes' are insufficient for a modify action that must introduce entirely new requirements into exception.md.

findings.description: The key requirements listed for crates/cclab-mamba/runtime/exception.md do not match the actual spec. The spec at that path has id: 'exception', with requirements R1 (class hierarchy), R2 (exception instantiation), R3 (exception matching), R4 (thread-local state), R5 (ExceptionGroup), R6 (except* syntax), R7 (symbol registration). None of these are about exception chaining. The reference context instead lists implementation details inferred from source code: 'MbException struct already has cause, context, suppress_context fields', 'store_exception_as_value populates __cause__/__context__/__suppress_context__', 'mb_raise_from (3 I64 args)', etc. These are source code observations, not spec requirements. The key requirements column is inaccurate for this entry.

The key requirements listed for crates/cclab-mamba/lower/hir-to-mir.md do not match the actual spec. The spec at that path has id: 'name-builtin-init' with requirements R10 (module-scope dunder variable initialization), R11 (REPL dunder initialization), R12 (symbol resolution for dunder stores) — all about __name__ initialization. The reference context instead describes Raise lowering dispatch logic ('HirStmt::Raise { value, from, .. } at ~line 1540: dispatches to mb_raise_from_with_context...') and notes a gap for 'raise existing_instance from Y'. None of this is in the spec. The key requirements are inaccurate.

findings.location: spec_plan — severely incomplete: only one entry
findings.description: The spec plan has a single entry (exception-chaining-runtime modifying exception.md) for a feature that touches at least three distinct areas. Missing entries: (a) crates/cclab-mamba/testing/conformance.md — listed as high-relevance with R7 correctly identified, but has no spec plan entry to reflect xfail removal and test passage for chaining_edge_cases.py; (b) traceback display — the reference context explicitly states 'Exception display / traceback chaining NOT YET DONE: no rendering of The above exception was the direct cause... or During handling of the above exception... messages; mb_traceback_format_exception in traceback_mod.rs does not follow __cause__/__context__ chain'. CPython 3.12 conformance requires traceback chaining display, but there is no spec plan entry for traceback_mod.rs or any traceback spec.

findings.location: spec_plan — sections 'overview, changes' insufficient for modify action adding new requirements
findings.description: The sole spec plan entry proposes modifying exception.md with sections 'overview, changes'. The actual exception.md spec has NO requirements about exception chaining at all — R1-R7 cover unrelated functionality. A modify action that must introduce entirely new requirements (mb_raise_from semantics, __cause__/__context__ attribute storage, suppress_context behavior, raise X from None semantics) needs at minimum a 'requirements' section and 'scenarios' or 'acceptance-criteria'. Sections 'overview, changes' are insufficient and will not produce a spec with traceable requirement IDs.

findings.location: reference_context.md — traceback display gap unaddressed in spec plan
findings.description: The reference context explicitly flags 'Exception display / traceback chaining NOT YET DONE' and names the specific source location (mb_traceback_format_exception in traceback_mod.rs). CPython 3.12 conformance for exception chaining includes printing 'The above exception was the direct cause of the following exception:' (for __cause__) and 'During handling of the above exception, another exception occurred:' (for __context__). This is a first-class part of the feature scope. The spec plan has no entry to spec this work, and there is no existing spec covering traceback_mod.rs. A new spec entry (or a modify entry if a traceback spec exists) is required.

findings.location: reference_context.md — raise X from existing_instance gap not tracked
findings.description: The reference context notes a correctness gap under hir-to-mir.md: 'For generic raise (raise existing_instance from Y): calls mb_raise_instance_with_context or mb_raise_instance — from clause is NOT passed to mb_raise_instance, a gap if raise-from is used with an existing instance.' This is a CPython 3.12 conformance bug — 'raise e from cause' where e is an already-constructed exception object should set __cause__. This gap is identified but not reflected in the spec plan as a requirement to fix.

The reference context describes parse_raise() behavior ('consumes raise keyword, then optionally parses value expr, then checks for from keyword') as key requirements for crates/cclab-mamba/parser/statements.md. However, the actual spec's R1 simple statement table does NOT list Raise. The R1 table covers: Return, Import, From-Import, Pass, Break, Continue, VarDecl, Assign, AugAssign, ExprStmt. Raise is absent from the spec requirements. The reference context is again describing source code behavior, not spec requirements.

findings.location: reference_context.md — behavioral_lang_tests T15.1/T15.2/T15.3 dependency not in spec plan
findings.description: The reference context correctly notes that behavioral_lang_tests.rs T15.1/T15.2/T15.3 remain #[ignore] because 'type(e.__cause__).__name__ requires type() builtin to return class name for ObjData::Exception — not yet wired'. This is a cross-cutting prerequisite: making these tests pass requires wiring type() dispatch for exception objects. This dependency is not tracked in the spec plan and is not surfaced as a prerequisite or blocker in the spec plan entry.

findings.location: reference_context.md — testing/conformance.md listed as high-relevance with no spec plan entry
findings.description: testing/conformance.md is listed at high relevance and correctly cites R7 for exception chaining. However, the spec plan has no entry for it despite needing to document the test state transition (xfail removed, fixture passes). At minimum, a modify entry should record that the xfail marker in chaining_edge_cases.py is removed and the conformance spec is updated to reflect R7 as satisfied.

findings.location: reference_context.md — value-and-rc.md and symbols.md key requirements describe code state, not spec
findings.description: For value-and-rc.md, the spec's R3 lists ObjData variants (Exception, Instance) without describing internal fields. The reference context describes 'cause and context fields inside MbException are Box<MbException>', 'ObjData::Instance { class_name, fields: HashMap<String, MbValue> }', etc. — these are source code details not in the spec. Similarly, symbols.md's key requirements describe what is 'already registered in symbols.rs' (mb_raise_from with 3 I64 args, etc.) rather than citing spec requirement IDs. Both are minor accuracy issues but indicate the key requirements column conflates spec content with source code observations throughout.

Correctly identifies R1 (Stmt::Raise listed as 'raise expr from expr') and R4 (Spanned<T> wrapping). Both verified accurate against the actual ast.md spec. The description 'both value and from are optional fields on the Raise variant' and span attachment are consistent with the spec.

Correctly identifies R1 (HirStmt::Raise listed in variants). The note 'No desugaring occurs for Raise in AST-to-HIR; the from field is preserved as-is through lowering' is correctly inferred from the spec (R2 lists what IS desugared, and Raise is absent). Accurate.

Correctly assigned low relevance. The spec's R1-R5 cover decorators, comprehensions, with-statements, for-else, and augmented assignments — none desugar Raise. The inference that 'Raise with from field is passed through unchanged' is consistent with the spec and assigned appropriate low relevance.

Relevance scores are broadly reasonable: exception.md and testing/conformance.md at high (directly implements/tests the feature), hir-to-mir/parser/hir/value-and-rc at medium (related compilation pipeline), ast-to-hir and symbols at low (background context). No clearly irrelevant specs included.

revisions_required: Fix runtime/exception.md key requirements: replace source code observations with accurate description of what IS in the spec (R1-R7: class hierarchy, instantiation, matching, thread-local state, ExceptionGroup, except*, symbol registration) and note that R_new requirements for exception chaining (mb_raise_from semantics, __cause__/__context__ storage, suppress_context) must be WRITTEN into the spec — they do not currently exist

Fix hir-to-mir.md key requirements: replace Raise-dispatch code observations with accurate description referencing the actual spec requirements R10 (module-scope dunder init), R11 (REPL dunder init), R12 (symbol resolution); note that Raise lowering support is present in source but has no corresponding spec requirement — new requirements must be added

Fix parser/statements.md key requirements: note that Raise is absent from R1's simple statement table and must be added to the spec; parse_raise() behavior is source code state, not a spec requirement

Add spec plan entry for traceback display: create or modify a traceback spec (covering mb_traceback_format_exception in traceback_mod.rs) to require following __cause__/__context__ chain and emitting 'The above exception was the direct cause...' and 'During handling of the above exception...' messages per CPython 3.12; sections: requirements, scenarios, changes
Add spec plan entry for crates/cclab-mamba/testing/conformance.md (modify): mark R7 xfail as removed, record that chaining_edge_cases.py fixture passes; sections: changes

Update exception-chaining-runtime spec plan entry sections from 'overview, changes' to 'requirements, scenarios, changes' to ensure new exception chaining requirements (mb_raise_from, mb_raise_with_context, mb_raise_from_with_context, mb_raise_instance_with_context, suppress_context semantics, raise X from None semantics) are formally documented with traceable R-IDs

Address the raise X from existing_instance gap in the spec plan: add a requirement to mb_raise_instance_with_context (or a new runtime function) that accepts an explicit cause argument so 'raise e from Y' works when e is an already-constructed exception object
